findings.title: cp00-090 self-evidence pack referenced by contract/validator but absent from this diff
findings.detail: The contract now registers current_subphase and a subphase_closeouts entry pointing at evidence_root 'docs/closeout-packs/cp00-090' and construction_inspection_ref 'docs/closeout-packs/cp00-090/construction-inspection.json' with production_ready_flag 'control_plane_hermes_validation_human_approval_marker_verified'. scripts/validate-rp00-control-plane-contract.mjs adds S11 to requiredClosedSubphases and requires inspection.subphase_id=='RP00.P08.M05.S11' plus inspection.control_plane_hermes_validation_human_approval_marker_verified===true, else it pushes 'construction inspection missing'/verify errors. This diff does not create construction-inspection.json (nor manifest/command-evidence/claude-review-result/adjudication) for cp00-090, and git status shows no such untracked directory. required_evidence_refs correctly point only at upstream cp00-089, so this is a self-evidence sequencing concern, not an upstream-binding defect.
findings.recommendation: Assemble the cp00-090 closeout pack (construction-inspection.json with subphase_id RP00.P08.M05.S11, human_acceptance_or_explicit_next_goal_boundary, and control_plane_hermes_validation_human_approval_marker_verified=true, plus manifest/command-evidence/claude-review-result/adjudication) and commit it alongside these changes before running the contract validator or finalizing closeout.

findings.title: completedSourceMicroPhaseIds appends RP00.P08.M05 without a uniqueness guard
findings.detail: In policies.js the policy builds completedSourceMicroPhaseIds as [...CP00-089 policy.completedSourceMicroPhaseIds, 'RP00.P08.M05']. The result (ending ...M04, M05) and the assembler's fail-closed requirement that upstream source_micro_phase_completed===false make a duplicate impossible today, but the validator only asserts .includes('RP00.P08.M05') and does not assert uniqueness, so a future change that added M05 to the upstream list would silently produce a duplicate id.
findings.recommendation: Optionally de-duplicate via a Set (mirroring the forbiddenBoundarySuiteClaims construction) or add a uniqueness assertion in assertControlPlaneHermesValidationHumanApprovalMarkerPolicy; non-blocking for this pack.

findings.title: Upstream entrypoint_id not explicitly validated though declared in fail_closed_on
findings.detail: fail_closed_on lists 'non_hermes_validation_human_approval_marker_entrypoint', and the output entrypoint_id is sourced from policy.acceptedEntrypointId. assembleControlPlaneHermesValidationHumanApprovalMarker does not inspect the upstream's entrypoint_id; correct upstream identity is instead enforced via pack_id/planned_pack_id==CP00-089, next_subphase==RP00.P08.M05.S11, the decision string, the boundary-flag block, and the full assertControlPlaneHermesValidationPermissionAuditBindingResult call. This matches the established pattern from prior packs and is sufficient, but the declared fail-closed reason has no direct runtime check.
findings.recommendation: No change required; if stricter symmetry is desired, add an explicit upstream entrypoint_id assertion. Informational only.

required_followups: Run npm run rp00:control-plane:validate after CP00-090 evidence files exist.
Continue to CP00-091 after commit; CP00-090 hands off to RP00.P08.M06.S01.
parsed_review.summary: CP00-090 correctly binds a metadata-only Human approval marker (RP00.P08.M05.S11) by consuming the CP00-089 permission-audit-binding result. The marker is explicitly declared not-an-approval, preserves human_approval_required_for_sensitive_actions, and forbids runtime approval creation, record writes, decision synthesis, and bypass. All runtime/permission/AuthZ/engine, audit, Hermes/command evidence, product-state, real-data/secret, and LDIP flags remain false. The assembler is fail-closed (upstream pack/handoff/decision/boundary checks plus full upstream result assertion, and any truthy boundary claim throws). It completes RP00.P08.M05 (phase_completed=false, open=[RP00.P08.M06]) and hands off to RP00.P08.M06.S01. Contract definition, synthetic fixture, policy, module, validators, and tests are mutually consistent with exact field-set and ordering assertions. No P0/P1 issues found; only a sequencing dependency on the cp00-090 evidence pack and two minor robustness notes.
